_disclaimer: SYNTHETIC DEMO DATA. These criteria are fabricated for demonstration and do NOT reproduce InterQual, MCG, or any licensed/copyrighted criteria content. They only mimic the STRUCTURE of medical-necessity criteria (criterion set -> requirements -> meets/does-not-meet) for demo purposes. Not for clinical or coverage use.

requirements.description: Documented conservative therapy of at least 6 weeks (e.g., physical therapy, NSAIDs, activity modification) prior to advanced imaging.
Documented neurologic finding (e.g., radiculopathy, positive straight-leg raise, dermatomal sensory/motor deficit).
No meaningful clinical improvement after conservative therapy, OR progressive/red-flag symptoms warranting expedited imaging.

criteria_sets.title: Cardiac Stress Testing - Nuclear (SPECT MPI)

requirements.description: Documented symptoms suggestive of ischemia (typical/atypical angina, anginal equivalent) OR intermediate/high pretest probability of CAD.
Resting ECG that is uninterpretable for ischemia OR patient unable to exercise (justifying pharmacologic/nuclear over standard exercise ECG).
Not performed within the routine screening interval for an asymptomatic low-risk patient.

red_flags_bypass: Acute coronary syndrome with ongoing chest pain

criteria_sets.title: Inpatient Admission vs. Observation - General Medical

requirements.description: Documented clinical need for hospital care with expectation of a stay crossing two midnights (inpatient), OR anticipated short stay < 2 midnights (observation).
Severity of illness: abnormal vitals/labs or acute condition requiring active inpatient-level management.
Intensity of service: treatment requiring the hospital setting (e.g., IV therapy, continuous monitoring, frequent reassessment).
